mainEntity.name: How do Idaho workers' comp laws differ from Utah for noise-induced hearing loss in Boise?
mainEntity.text: How do Idaho workers' comp laws differ from Utah for noise-induced hearing loss in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Idaho follows federal OSHA but lacks Utah's specific industrial noise statutes (34A-2-501 et seq.); claims use general occupational disease rules with no 180-day report mandate, emphasizing medical evidence of causation during employment.

suggestedAnswer.text: Unlike Utah's codified hearing loss rules with strict reporting, Idaho treats NIHL as occupational disease under Idaho Code §72-1001, requiring proof of work causation without unique deadlines. Boise workers face similar OSHA thresholds but varying insurer scrutiny. Cross-state commuters need jurisdiction-specific advice; Utah firms like Just Work Comp may refer Idaho cases.[1]

name: Utah vs Idaho Workplace Hearing Loss Comp Laws
description: Idaho follows federal OSHA but lacks Utah's specific industrial noise statutes (34A-2-501 et seq.); claims use general occupational disease rules with no 180-da
